resources.string: Choose or check a server, then open project selection.
This app is an OpenCode client only. It does not create or run an OpenCode Server. Start one on a computer or server, make sure this device can reach it, then add the connection.
Delete server "%1$s"? This removes the server config and locally saved credentials.
Passwords, SSH private keys, STCP tokens, and secret keys are saved in Keystore and never shown in plain text.
Leave sensitive credentials blank to keep existing values. New input overwrites locally saved Keystore credentials.
Failed to read private key file
SSH private key must be %1$d KiB or smaller
Use the OpenCode service URL directly without creating a local tunnel.
Access the remote OpenCode service through local SSH port forwarding.
Access the remote OpenCode service through a frps STCP visitor bound to a local port.
Access the remote OpenCode service through an SSH tunnel.
Recommended. Use an OpenSSH private key without saving an SSH password.
Log in with the SSH account password. The password is only saved in local Keystore.
Provide both password and private key for servers that require combined authentication.
Paste private key content directly. It will not be shown in plain text after saving.
Read once from a local phone file, then save only the key content and file name.
Save the host fingerprint on first connection; reject later connections if it changes.
Manually enter a known host fingerprint for servers managed with existing ops fingerprints.
The first connection saves the host fingerprint; later connections are rejected if the fingerprint changes.
Compatible with the default frp protocol.
Use when the frp deployment explicitly enables the newer protocol.
STCP tokens and secret keys are saved only in Keystore. This first version supports local-port mode only.

This direct server uses HTTP on a non-loopback address with an OpenCode username and password. Android Keystore protects only credentials stored on this device; it does not encrypt HTTP traffic. Network intermediaries may read the password, project content, prompts, and attachments. Save anyway?
No file selected. After reading it, only the private key content is saved; the file path is not stored.
Delete recent project "%1$s"? This only removes the local record and does not delete server project files.
Enter the project path on the machine running OpenCode server.
Server project path cannot be empty
This file exceeds the in-app preview size limit.
This file is too large, has too many lines, or contains an excessively long line for in-app preview.
This file has no displayable content
Preview is not available for this binary file.
This image is too large to decode in the app.
You can add up to %1$d project files
The project file path is invalid. Choose the file again.
Unable to open the project. Try again.
Close project "%1$s"? This only exits the current project view and will not delete the server project or recent project record.
The current server did not return a project ID, so the project name cannot be edited
MCP %1$s needs authentication. Android has not integrated the auth flow yet.
Prompt capabilities are still loading. Try again shortly.
Prompt capabilities changed. Confirm the current model and agent, then try again.
The selected model is no longer valid or available. Select another model.
The selected agent is unavailable. Select Build or Plan.
The selected reasoning level is no longer valid. Select it again.
A send or stop operation is already running for this session
The request result is uncertain, but the server may have accepted the message. Check the session before retrying.
Sending a new message will replace the reverted content.
Track, review, and revert changes in this project
Access files outside the project directory
Modify files, including edits, writes, patches, and multi-edits
The model is waiting for your confirmation
Use the current model and provider default reasoning settings.
Use the %1$s reasoning settings for the current model.
Add local phone attachments or project file context from the current server project.
Use the Android file picker and convert the file to an attachment part before sending.
Browse or search the current server project and send files as context.
This file type is not supported yet
Attachment is too large. Each file must be %1$d MiB or smaller
You can add up to %1$d attachments
Attachments cannot exceed %1$d MiB in total
Attachment data is invalid or damaged. Choose the file again.
LSPs are auto-detected from file types
Authentication required. Auth flow is not supported yet.
Authentication failed. Check the server credentials.
Too many requests. Try again later.
Unable to reach the server. Check the network and server status.
The server connection timed out. Try again.
The server returned an invalid response.
The server response exceeds the app size limit.
The server connection changed. Try again.
The local port is already in use. Choose another port and try again.
The STCP component is unavailable in this app build. Install a build with native STCP support.
Unable to complete this operation. Check the server configuration.
Project snapshot does not match the current connection
Server URL must be an HTTP or HTTPS URL with a valid host
Server URL cannot contain a username or password
Server URL cannot contain query parameters
Server URL cannot contain a fragment
The save result is uncertain. Refresh and confirm before retrying.
SSH private key cannot be empty
SSH host fingerprint cannot be empty
Connect timeout must be greater than 0
KeepAlive interval must be greater than 0
STCP server name cannot be empty
STCP secret key cannot be empty
STCP local bind port must be 1-65535
Language, permissions, display, appearance, and notifications.
Check notifications, battery policy, and recent task retention to reduce connection interruptions in the background.
Keep an entry for external keyboard shortcuts; search and editing will be added later.
Manage OpenCode servers, health checks, and login information.
Connect, disconnect, or create custom providers; keys are never shown in plain text.
Enable or hide models grouped by provider.
OC Deck v%1$s, project links, licenses, and notices.
App-level display and appearance settings. Current server: %1$s
Choose the display language for OC Deck. Current: %1$s
Change the display language for OC Deck. Follow system uses the current Android language.
Choose whether OC Deck follows the system, uses light mode, or uses dark mode. Current: %1$s
System follows Android dark mode; light and dark keep this app fixed to that appearance.
Show system notifications when the agent finishes or needs attention.
Show system notifications when permission is required.
Show system notifications when an error occurs.
Play sound when the agent is complete or needs attention.
Play sound when a permission is required.
Play sound when an error occurs.
Choose the sound for this alert. Selecting None turns off sound for this category.
Alerts for session completion, permission requests, questions, and errors.
Alerts for completed sessions and questions awaiting an answer.
Alerts for permission requests awaiting a response.
Use the current Android system language
Use English for the app interface
Use Simplified Chinese for the app interface
Keep session events, SSH forwarding, and permission alerts more stable in the background.
Keep session events, SSH forwarding, and permission alerts more stable in the background. Current server: %1$s
These settings only affect how Android schedules this app. They do not modify OpenCode server configuration.
Used for permission requests, question confirmations, and long-running task results. Notification text avoids sensitive credentials.
Without notification permission, some background alerts are only visible after returning to the app.
Notification permission not granted. You can request it again later.
This Android version does not require separate notification permission.
Reduces the chance that background SSE, SSH forwarding, and long-running tasks are paused by the system.
Battery saving policies may delay or interrupt background connections.
This system does not support requesting battery optimization exemption directly.
Unable to open the battery optimization confirmation dialog.
If your device offers "lock app" or "keep recent task", pin OC Deck to avoid one-tap cleanup closing it.
Entry points vary by vendor. Android has no unified permission dialog for this.
Server: %1$s. Provider keys only show connection state and are never displayed in plain text.
The server returned no manageable providers.
Credentials are managed by server environment variables and cannot be disconnected here.
This provider is managed by server configuration and must currently be disabled there.
Enter a generic API key. Providers requiring extra fields or OAuth use their corresponding authentication flow.
The key is sent directly to OpenCode Server and is not stored or shown in the page, logs, or ordinary local settings.
Loading authentication methods available for this project...
Complete all currently visible authentication fields
Waiting for OpenCode Server to finish authorization. You can cancel at any time; refresh afterward to confirm the state.

This link uses localhost or a loopback address. If OpenCode Server runs on another device, the phone browser usually cannot reach that callback. Prefer a device-code or manual-code method, or configure secure port forwarding.
Unable to open the authorization link safely
Failed to create OAuth authorization flow
OAuth credentials were saved and project capability reload was requested.
The OAuth operation was cancelled, but the server may still have completed it. Refresh to confirm the current state.
The OAuth callback outcome is uncertain. Do not submit it again immediately; refresh first to confirm the current state.
Delete the authentication saved for %1$s on OpenCode Server? Existing configuration or environment variables may still keep it loaded.
The target is a non-loopback HTTP server. The API key will not be protected by TLS in transit, even if local storage is protected.
Provider credentials were saved and project capability reload was requested.
Provider authentication was deleted and project capability reload was requested.
The authentication change was committed, but server instance refresh failed. Refresh this page to confirm the current state.
The authentication change was committed, but the provider list could not be reloaded.
Configure provider ID, Base URL, API key, models, and request headers.
Server: %1$s. Configuration is saved to OpenCode Server; keys and header values are never shown again.
Current state: disabled; saving will re-enable it
Leave this blank while editing to retain current server authentication, or enter a new value to replace the API key.
Server PATCH cannot reliably delete existing models, so a saved model ID cannot be renamed or removed. Its display name can still be updated.
OpenCode Server deep-merges configuration. To avoid pretending this is a full replacement, saved model IDs and header names remain read-only.
Header values are sensitive. Saved values are never loaded into this page; leave a value blank to retain it.
Leave blank to retain the saved value
A saved header name cannot be removed or renamed. Enter a value to replace it, or leave it blank to retain it.

Saving first stages the configuration as disabled, writes new credentials, and only then enables it. If a step fails, the page reports disabled or unknown state instead of misreporting a partial commit as a complete failure.

The provider is disabled in server configuration before saved authentication is removed best-effort. The configuration definition remains because the server deep-merge API cannot reliably delete it, allowing later editing or re-enabling.
Send Custom Credentials Over Cleartext HTTP
The target is a non-loopback HTTP server. An API key or new header values will not be protected by TLS in transit. Confirming submits only the currently validated, frozen form snapshot.
The custom provider was saved and enabled, and project capability reload was requested.
The custom provider was saved and enabled, but server instance refresh failed. Return to Providers to confirm its state.
The configuration was safely retained in a disabled state, but credential storage failed. Review the error, re-enter credentials, and save again.
The server commit outcome is unknown. Secret inputs were cleared. Do not submit again immediately; return to Providers and refresh first.
The custom provider was disabled and saved authentication was cleared.
The custom provider is disabled, but authentication cleanup failed. It will not be loaded; retry cleanup later.
The custom provider was disabled and credentials were cleared, but server instance refresh failed.
The disable outcome is unknown. Return to Providers and refresh before trying again.
This custom provider does not exist on the server or is not a compatible OpenAI-style custom configuration.
Provider ID must contain 1–128 lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores. A saved ID cannot be changed.
Display name is required and cannot exceed 200 characters.
Base URL must be HTTP(S) with a valid host and no user info, query, or fragment.
The API key is too long.
No more than 100 models are allowed.
Model IDs and names must contain 1–256 characters.
No more than 50 headers are allowed.
A header name or value is invalid. New headers require a value.
Header names must be unique, ignoring case.
No manageable models. Connect a provider first.
OC Deck is an OpenCode client for Android focused on mobile project and session workflows.
OC Deck is an independent community client. It is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by OpenCode or Anomaly.

OC Deck source code is licensed under the MIT License. The LICENSE, NOTICE, third-party notices, and trademark statement below are read offline from resources bundled in the APK. Third-party components remain subject to their respective license terms.
This legal text is not included in the current APK.
Unable to open the link. Check that a browser is available on this device.

string-array.name: prompt_examples
string-array.item: Fix a TODO in this codebase
What is the tech stack of this project?
Find and fix a security vulnerability
Add unit tests for the user service
Refactor this function to make it easier to read
How should I deploy this project?
Review my code and suggest best practices
Add error handling to this function
Help me write a migration script
How do the environment variables work here?
